questions.text: How many states does India have (as of recent count)?
questions.explanation: India has 28 states (and 8 union territories).

questions.text: Which is the national anthem of India?
questions.explanation: 'Jana Gana Mana' is the national anthem of India.

questions.text: The currency of India is the ____.
questions.explanation: India's currency is the Rupee.

questions.text: Hindi is written in the Devanagari script.
questions.explanation: Hindi uses the Devanagari script. True.

questions.text: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Mars is called the Red Planet.

questions.text: Which is the largest planet in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is the largest planet.

questions.text: The ____ is the natural satellite of the Earth.
questions.explanation: The Moon is the Earth's natural satellite.

questions.text: Plants need sunlight to make their food.
questions.explanation: Plants need sunlight for photosynthesis. True.

questions.text: Which is the largest animal in the world?
questions.explanation: The blue whale is the largest animal on Earth.

questions.text: Which bird cannot fly?
questions.explanation: The ostrich is a bird that cannot fly.

questions.text: A group of lions is called a ____.
questions.explanation: A group of lions is called a pride.

questions.text: Honey is made by bees.
questions.explanation: Bees make honey. True.

questions.text: Who was the first Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru was India's first Prime Minister.

questions.text: ____ Ambedkar is known as the Father of the Indian Constitution.
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar is the chief architect of the Indian Constitution.

questions.text: Mother Teresa worked to help poor and sick people.
questions.explanation: Mother Teresa served the poor and sick. True.

questions.text: Which is the highest mountain range in India?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas are the highest mountain range.

questions.text: The ____ Ocean is on the south of India.
questions.explanation: The Indian Ocean lies to the south of India.

questions.text: Republic Day is celebrated on 26th January.
questions.explanation: Republic Day is on 26 January. True.

questions.text: Which month has 29 days in a leap year?
questions.explanation: February has 29 days in a leap year.

questions.text: There are ____ days in a leap year.
questions.explanation: A leap year has 366 days.

questions.text: There are 24 hours in a day.
questions.explanation: A day has 24 hours. True.

questions.text: Which sense organ do we use to hear?
questions.explanation: We hear with our ears.

questions.text: Ice melts and becomes ____.
questions.explanation: Ice melts into water.

questions.text: A magnet attracts iron objects.
questions.explanation: Magnets attract things made of iron. True.

questions.text: In which sport do we use a racket and a shuttlecock?
questions.explanation: Badminton uses a racket and a shuttlecock.

questions.text: ____ Chand was a legendary Indian hockey player.
questions.explanation: Major Dhyan Chand was a hockey legend.

questions.text: The Olympic Games are held every 4 years.
questions.explanation: The Olympics happen every four years. True.

questions.text: What is the national aquatic animal of India?
questions.explanation: The Ganges river dolphin is India's national aquatic animal.

questions.text: The national emblem of India shows ____ lions.
questions.explanation: The Lion Capital of Ashoka shows four lions (three visible).

questions.text: We should not talk to strangers and share personal details.
questions.explanation: Being careful with strangers keeps us safe. True.

questions.text: Which number do we call for fire emergency in India?
questions.explanation: 101 is the fire emergency number in India.
